title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: Findings: - High: [auto-push-after-commit.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L27) decides commit success by grepping `stdout` only, then [auto-push-after-commit.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L35) unconditionally runs `git push` on any tracked branch. If `git commit` fails via exit code or `stderr`, this hook can still publish the previous `HEAD`. It also creates an external publication side effect without explicit intent. - High: The diff includes raw telemetry/session artifacts that expose task history and operational details, e.g. [agent-memory/experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/agent-memory/experience.jsonl#L22) and [bookkeeping/experience.jsonl](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/bookkeeping/experience.jsonl#L1). These contain task summaries, commands, URLs, and SSH context; they are privacy-sensitive and will add large non-deterministic churn if committed. - Medium: [gha-node24-migrate.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/scripts/gha-node24-migrate.sh#L131) stashes, rebases, and pops with `|| true`, suppressing conflicts and failures. The script can then [gha-node24-migrate.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/scripts/gha-node24-migrate.sh#L136) commit/push workflow edits on top of a broken or partially restored worktree, or leave the caller’s local changes in an unexpected state with no signal. - Medium: [eval-skill.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L341) removed the old prose/CJK filtering inside fenced shell blocks, but still treats remaining lines as candidate binaries at [eval-skill.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L373). Japanese explanatory lines inside valid ```bash examples will now be flagged as `PHANTOM_CMD`, skewing scores and causing false failures. Residual risk: I did not find obvious hardcoded secrets in the executable hunks, but the worktree is noisy enough that I would avoid committing the generated `experience.jsonl` / `golden-store` / `correction-pairs` artifacts without an explicit retention policy.
